latest.title: Result of General Meeting
latest.content:
Today 15:15
RNS Number : 2669J
Verici Dx PLC
22 June 2026
Verici Dx plc
("
Verici Dx
" or the "
Company
")
Result of General Meeting
Verici Dx Plc, (AIM: VRCI),
a developer of advanced clinical diagnostics for organ transplant,
announces that at the General Meeting ("GM") held earlier today, all resolutions, as set out in the GM notice published on 5 June 2026, were duly passed on a poll. Resolution 1 was passed as an ordinary resolution and resolution 2 was passed as a special resolution.
The voting results of the GM were as follows:
In Favour
Against
Withheld
Resolution
Votes
%
Votes
%
Votes
1
855,487,726
99.78
1,862,886
0.22
11,505
2
855,484,473
99.78
1,866,139
0.22
11,505
Admission and Total Voting Rights
Application has been made to the London Stock Exchange for admission of the 700,000,000 Placing Shares and 53,345,832 WRAP Retail Offer Shares ("Admission"). It is expected that Admission will become effective and that trading will commence in the Placing Shares and WRAP Retail Offer Shares at 8.00 a.m. on 23 June 2026. Additionally, an adviser to the Company has agreed to have issued to it 20,571,428 new Ordinary Shares (the "Adviser Shares") at the Issue Price in lieu of fees that would otherwise have been owed to it. It is expected that Admission of the Adviser Shares will also become effective at 8.00 a.m. on 23 June 2026 and trading in such shares will commence at such time.
Following Admission, the total issued share capital of the Company will consist of 2,287,311,387 Ordinary Shares. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 2,287,311,387. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by Shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
The new Ordinary Shares will be fully paid and will rank pari passu in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company.
Capitalised terms not defined have the same meaning as given to them in the Company's Circular dated 5 June 2026.
Enquires:
Verici Dx plc
www.vericidx.com
Sara Barrington, CEO
Julian Baines, Chairman
Via Walbrook PR

Alice Woodings / Rachel Broad
Mob: +44 (0)7407 804 654 / +44 (0)7747 515 393
Verici Dx
Verici Dx plc (AIM: VRCI) is a precision diagnostics company transforming care for transplant patients. The company combines multiomic analysis with proprietary artificial intelligence to deliver predictive, actionable, data-driven intelligence that reflects the complexity and heterogeneity of transplant patients, enabling clinicians to optimize therapy, guide biopsy decisions, and stratify risk with greater confidence.
Operating at the intersection of laboratory and data science, Verici Dx develops complex models that answer the clinical questions that matter most with unrivaled clarity and precision. All tests are built to rigorous scientific standards, validated across inclusive, and real-world patient populations to ensure clinical relevance and reliability. Verici Dx's lead product, Tutivia™, is a post-kidney transplant test focused on early detection of acute rejection.
The company is UK headquartered in Cardiff for the UK, and in Franklin, Tennessee for the U.S. For more information, please visit
https://vericidx.com/
and follow us on
LinkedIn
.

latest.title: Result of WRAP Retail Offer
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0978I
Verici Dx PLC
12 June 2026
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, OR INTO OR WITHIN THE UNITED STATES, AUSTRALIA, NEW ZEALAND, CANADA, SOUTH AFRICA OR JAPAN, OR ANY MEMBER STATE OF THE EEA, OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE, OR TO ANY OTHER PERSON TO WHOM, TO DO SO MIGHT CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OR BREACH OF ANY APPLICABLE LAW OR REGULATION. PLEASE SEE THE IMPORTANT NOTICE AT THE END OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT.
12 June 2026
Verici Dx plc
("
Verici Dx
" or the "
Company
")
Result of WRAP Retail Offer
Verici Dx Plc, (AIM: VRCI), a developer of advanced clinical diagnostics for organ transplant, confirms, further to the announcements made on 8 June 2026, the result of its WRAP Retail Offer at the Issue Price of 0.35 pence per share. The Company announces that it has raised aggregate gross proceeds of approximately £0.19 million pursuant to the WRAP Retail Offer in addition to the previously announced Placing. Accordingly, the Company will issue a total of 53,345,832 WRAP Retail Offer Shares.
In total, the Placing and the WRAP Retail Offer has raised gross proceeds of approximately £2.6 million for the Company, via the Placing of 700,000,000 Placing Shares and the 53,345,832 WRAP Retail Offer Shares.
For the avoidance of doubt, the WRAP Retail Offer is not part of the Placing. The issue of the WRAP Retail Offer Shares is conditional upon:
• the passing of certain resolutions to be put to shareholders of Verici Dx plc at a General Meeting, which is expected to be held at Shoosmiths LLP's London office at 1 Bow Churchyard, London EC4M 9DQ at 11.30 a.m. on 22 June 2026; and
• the new Ordinary Shares being admitted to trading on AIM.
Terms used but not defined in this announcement have the same meaning as set out in the Company's announcement released at 7am on 8 June 2026.
Verici Dx plc
Sara Barrington, CEO
www.vericidx.com
Julian Baines, Chairman
Singer Capital Markets (Nominated Adviser, Joint Broker & Joint Bookrunner)

MF is acting exclusively for the Company and for no-one else and will not regard any other person (whether or not a recipient of this announcement) as its client in relation to the Retail Offer and will not be responsible to anyone other than the Company for providing the protections afforded to its clients, nor for providing advice in connection with the Retail Offer, Admission and the other arrangements referred to in this announcement.

No statement in this announcement is intended to be a profit forecast and no statement in this announcement should be interpreted to mean that earnings or target dividend per share of the Company for the current or future financial years would necessarily match or exceed the historical published earnings or dividends per share of the Company.
Neither the content of the Company's website (or any other website) nor the content of any website accessible from hyperlinks on the Company's website (or any other website) is incorporated into or forms part of this announcement. The Ordinary Shares to be issued or sold pursuant to the Retail Offer will not be admitted to trading on any stock exchange other than the London Stock Exchange.

latest.title: Publication of Circular & Notice of GM
latest.content:
Today 13:52
RNS Number : 4424H
Verici Dx PLC
08 June 2026
Verici Dx plc
("Verici Dx" or the "Company")
Publication of Circular
& Notice of General Meeting
Verici Dx Plc, (AIM: VRCI),
a developer of advanced clinical diagnostics for organ transplant,
announces that a Circular has been published containing a notice of a general meeting to be held at the offices of
Shoosmiths LLP at 1 Bow Churchyard, London, EC4M 9DQ
at 11:30 a.m. on 22 June 2026 ("
General Meeting
").
The General Meeting is being held to ensure that the Company has sufficient authority to issue and allot new Ordinary Shares
on a non-pre-emptive basis following the Fundraising announced on 5 June 2026.
The Circular and Notice of General Meeting has been posted to Shareholders and is available on the Company's website at:
https://vericidx.com/investors/documents/
Unless otherwise defined, all definitions used in this announcement will have the same meaning as described in the Circular.

Verici Dx plc (AIM: VRCI) is a precision diagnostics company transforming care for transplant patients. The company combines multiomic analysis with proprietary artificial intelligence to deliver predictive, actionable, data-driven intelligence that reflects the complexity and heterogeneity of transplant patients, enabling clinicians to optimize therapy, guide biopsy decisions, and stratify risk with greater confidence.
Operating at the intersection of laboratory and data science, Verici Dx develops complex models that answer the clinical questions that matter most with unrivaled clarity and precision. All tests are built to rigorous scientific standards, validated across inclusive, and real-world patient populations to ensure clinical relevance and reliability. Verici Dx's lead product, Tutivia™, is a post-kidney transplant test focused on early detection of acute rejection.
The company is UK headquartered in Cardiff for the UK, and in Franklin, Tennessee for the U.S. For more information, please visit

company_30s.what_they_do: VRCI Ltd. builds and sells diagnostic assays that help clinicians detect rare genetic disorders early. Their core product line uses a proprietary micro‑array platform that can screen dozens of genes in a single test.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Rare‑disease diagnostics is a growing field as more families seek definitive answers. The company’s platform offers faster turnaround and lower costs than the current gold‑standard tests, giving it a competitive edge in a market that is still fragmented.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the early commercial phase, relying on a small pipeline of clinical studies. It has recently closed a £2.5 million placing to shore up cash and has announced a WRAP retail offer to attract smaller investors. The stock is trading at a fraction of its 52‑week high, reflecting the market’s uncertainty about the commercial viability of its assays.

rns_narrative: VRCI has just raised £2.5 million through a placing and is actively courting retail investors with a limited‑time offer. The company’s board is restructuring, and a recent general meeting approved a new strategic plan that focuses on accelerating the regulatory approval of its flagship test.

The latest RNS also highlighted a study validating the economic benefits of the company’s proprietary test, which could support a pricing strategy that appeals to payers. Together, these moves signal a pivot from a purely research‑focused model to a revenue‑generating one.
Despite the fundraising, the firm remains cash‑burning, with a monthly burn of roughly £500k. The upcoming Q2 update will be critical to see if the new capital translates into measurable progress.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical chatter – investors are wary that the company’s recent fundraising is a last‑ditch effort to stay afloat.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to the study that validates cost savings and the new board’s experience in biotech commercialization. They believe the company could finally unlock its product pipeline.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most voices warn that the firm’s cash burn is unsustainable, that its tests have yet to receive regulatory approval, and that the WRAP offer is a sign of desperation rather than confidence.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has stayed consistently negative; a few bullish posts appeared after the placing announcement, but they have not yet swayed the broader community.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for VRCI is essentially silent, indicating a lack of public interest and a high potential for surprise if a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: A regulatory filing that moves the flagship assay closer to approval could trigger a price jump.
The next Q2 earnings report will reveal whether the new capital has accelerated product development or merely extended the burn.
If the WRAP retail offer reaches its £200k cap, the influx of new shares could provide a temporary liquidity boost.
Any partnership announcement with a large diagnostics provider would signal market confidence.
expect_next.watch_for: Key data points over the next 2–3 months: (1) progress reports on regulatory milestones, (2) actual cash burn versus forecast, (3) share volume spikes around the WRAP offer, and (4) any third‑party validation or pricing agreements.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a deep capitulation. A crash‑bottom signal would need to be confirmed by a sharp drop followed by a clear accumulation zone—none of which is evident yet. If a crash bottom is identified, the APEX score (currently high) suggests a tight retracement zone that could be exploited.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the firm can demonstrate regulatory progress and secure a partnership, the price could rebound to a level halfway between the current trading price and the 52‑week high. Given the current valuation, that would represent roughly a 4‑5× upside. The deep‑crash nature of the setup implies that the upside is asymmetric – small losses are offset by the potential for a large rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The primary risk is that the company may run out of cash before achieving a regulatory milestone, leading to a further decline or even a forced sale of assets. If the burn rate continues, the worst‑case scenario is a price drop to zero, wiping out the entire investment.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~5× current price. Potential downside: essentially 100 % if the company collapses. The risk‑reward ratio is therefore highly skewed in favour of the upside, making it a classic contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear confirmation of a bottom—ideally a 3‑day consolidation at the lowest point followed by a volume spike. Allocate 10–15 % of discretionary capital, set a stop 10 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout, and plan to trail the stop as the price moves higher.
playbook.if_watching: Trigger a small position if the company announces a regulatory milestone or if the WRAP offer reaches 75 % of its target and volume surges. Use the 52‑week low as a psychological support level; if the price holds above it for a week, consider a larger stake.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or is delisted, exit immediately. No further positions should be taken in that scenario.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VRCI is a deep‑crash, high‑potential biotech that could rebound dramatically once it shows regulatory traction, but its survival hinges on converting recent fundraising into measurable progress.
